block.caption: FIGURE 1.4 Some of the model systems used to study developmental biology. From left to right the silhouettes represent the following model organisms: Arabidopsis thaliana, Drosophila melanogaster, Hydra vulgaris, Caenorhabditis elegans, Xenopus laevis, Danio rerio, Gallus gallus, Mus musculus, and stem cells of Homo sapiens (blastocyst with inner cell mass depicted).
